mode_selected: Mode 2 — Infrastructure & FUSE (with critical Mode 1 finding promoted to top)
mode_reason: Two dispatcher triggers fired: (a) disk project-footprint at 940MB, well past the 500MB warning threshold; (b) one stranded patch (STRANDED-EXP-290) >24h old. Mode 2 selected because the disk warning is the more actionable infrastructure issue. While executing Mode 2's Step 1 freshness check, the FUSE↔GitHub md5 spot-check passed but the deeper 'state hygiene' step (Step 5) attempted to count wins and discovered data/wins.json fails to parse — a critical infrastructure incident that takes priority over everything else in this report.

modes_checked.operator_directive_preflight: NO PENDING DIRECTIVES (20 directive files in monitor/tinker/operator-directives/, all status:open or status:completed; none promoted to status:pending).
modes_checked.mode_1_pipeline_health: AMBER. Output freshness all green: integrity 1h, status 1h, expansion-tracker 2h, latest-poll-summary 3h, curmudgeon tracker 3h, decider daily-report 5h. STRANDED-EXP-290-generate-html now 25h old (just past 24h flag threshold) — operator hasn't applied build-scripts/generate-html.js patches yet.
modes_checked.mode_2_infrastructure_fuse: RED. (a) FUSE↔GitHub md5 check on 13 key files: all match — sync direction is fine. (b) git auth: 200 OK. (c) DISK: project footprint 940MB (≥500MB warning). Three abandoned /tmp clones consuming 643MB combined (21-33h dir-mtime, no active locks). (d) CRITICAL — discovered during Step 5 state hygiene: data/wins.json fails JSON.parse at byte 418561 / line 1411 col 2374 on both FUSE AND origin/main. Bisect shows commit d09d978 ('Workspace sync: 2026-05-08T02:05:18Z') introduced the breakage; predecessor 6e68bba parsed cleanly with 71 wins.
modes_checked.mode_3_cost_engineering: Skipped this run — Mode 2 + critical incident took priority. Last cost-engineering pass: 2026-05-07T02-40 run.
modes_checked.mode_4_proposals_and_fixes: Authored 1 stranded-patches file (wins.json operator action) and 1 PROP (PROP-024 workspace-sync JSON validity gate).

imminent_failures.expected_failure: Will throw JSON.parse error on data/wins.json read after fresh clone. Decider's analyst-attention-inbox processing, issue-creation, and patch-self-apply all read wins.json.
Same fail mode — clones fresh, reads wins.json, throws.
Cross-checks wins count vs GitHub raw URL pre-Mode-0 — both copies will fail to parse.

findings.description: data/wins.json on origin/main is unparseable as JSON. node test.js fails the very first check; node build.js html fails inside printTally at build.js:184. Decider, curmudgeon, analyst will all throw on next run.

evidence.root_cause: An HTML <span class="archive-cite"> wrapper with a nested <a href="https://..."> attribute was added to the WIN-064 detail_extra string. The four bare " characters inside class="..." and href="..." were not escaped to \" before the JSON value was committed.
evidence.verified_repair: Tested build-scripts/fix-json-quotes.js on a copy at /sessions/friendly-relaxed-cori/mnt/outputs/wins-test.json: 'fixed 4 unescaped quotes in 4 passes'. Resulting file parses; 71 wins recovered; byte-delta = +4. The repair is mechanically correct and minimal.
evidence.test_js_output: ── 1. wins.json Schema ──  FAIL: wins.json parses as valid JSON: Expected ',' or '}' after property value in JSON at position 418561 (line 1411 column 2374)
evidence.build_js_output: SyntaxError at printTally (build.js:184:21) — same parse error

suggested_fix.operator_one_liner: cd <clone> && git pull origin main && node build-scripts/fix-json-quotes.js data/wins.json && node test.js && node build.js html && git add data/wins.json docs/index.html && git commit -m 'Fix unescaped quotes in WIN-064 detail_extra (archive-cite span)' && git push
suggested_fix.rationale: fix-json-quotes.js is the existing tool authored for this exact class of bug. Tinker is not permitted to modify wins.json directly per CLAUDE.md hard rule ('Don't duplicate the decider. ... Never patch wins.json or sections.json.'), so the fix is staged as an operator action.

findings.preventive_summary: PROP-024 proposes a pre-push JSON-validity gate in workspace-sync that would have refused to commit d09d978's wins.json (and would optionally have auto-repaired via fix-json-quotes.js, bounded at ≤32 bytes inserted).

findings.description: STRANDED-EXP-290-generate-html (3 patches to build-scripts/generate-html.js for Timestamp Error tab) has been waiting on operator manual application for 25h, just past the 24h flag threshold.
findings.evidence: monitor/decisions/stranded-patches-2026-05-07T01-19-55Z.json file mtime = 25h ago. monitor/decisions/applied-stranded-patches/ contains only README.md.

suggested_fix.operator_action: cd to clone with direct push, apply the three find/replace patches in build-scripts/generate-html.js, run node build.js html && node test.js, git add + commit + push, then move the stranded file to monitor/decisions/applied-stranded-patches/
suggested_fix.rationale: EXP-290 represents committed analyst work that hasn't reached the rendered page yet.

findings.description: Project-induced disk footprint is 940MB, well past the 500MB warning. Three abandoned clones in /tmp account for 643MB: /tmp/edit-clone (241MB, dir-mtime 21h), /tmp/prop017-clone (201MB, dir-mtime 33h), /tmp/check-clone (201MB, dir-mtime 21h). No active git locks, no recent file mtime activity. Headed toward the 1000MB CRITICAL threshold.
findings.evidence: du -sm /tmp/*-clone /tmp/dome-* yields 241/201/201 for the three clones plus the 104MB clone this run created. ls of .git/ in each abandoned clone shows last activity 21-33h ago; no .git/index.lock or HEAD.lock present.

suggested_fix.operator_command: rm -rf /tmp/edit-clone /tmp/prop017-clone /tmp/check-clone  # frees 643MB; verified no active locks and no file-mtime activity in last hour
suggested_fix.rationale: Tinker does not auto-rm clones it didn't create. Conservative approach — operator confirms no in-flight session is using these. After cleanup the project footprint drops to ~387MB, well below the warning threshold.

findings.note: tinker's own dome-tinker-clone in /tmp will be cleaned at end of this run.

findings.description: CLAUDE.md (lines 131-133) and monitor/prompts/tinker.md (lines 19-21) still list dome-integrity / dome-tinker / dome-social as 'Daily 9 AM / 10:30 AM / 11 AM' (and tinker.md '~9/~10:30/~11'). Actual cron expressions are 0 2 * * * (integrity = 02:08 AM), 30 3 * * * (tinker = 03:35 AM), 30 4 * * * (social = 04:30 AM). Previous tinker run (2026-05-07T02-40) flagged this and authored a stranded find/replace patch; the patch has not been applied.
findings.evidence: list_scheduled_tasks shows the actual cron expressions; grep of CLAUDE.md / tinker.md still shows the old labels; previous run's recommendation #1 was 'Apply stranded self-fix STRANDED-TINKER-SCHEDULE-FIX-2026-05-07' but no such file is present in monitor/decisions/ today (likely either operator deleted without applying, or it was never written to FUSE in a way that persisted — workspace-sync rules around NEVER_PUSH for monitor/prompts/*.md and CLAUDE.md may have been a factor).

suggested_fix.rationale: Both files are git-owned (NEVER_PUSH from FUSE) so the patch needs operator's clone with direct push.

findings.description: PROP-020 and PROP-021 carry tautological verification_patterns. Each just tests that the PROP file exists with a 'verification_pattern' field — i.e., always returns FIXED whether or not the underlying optimization was implemented. This is the exact PROP-003 failure mode the lifecycle reference warns about.

evidence.PROP-020: verification_pattern = 'test -f monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-020-drift-audit-split.json && grep -q \'"verification_pattern"\' monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-020-drift-audit-split.json && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN'
evidence.PROP-021: verification_pattern = 'test -f monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-021-curmudgeon-dispatcher-optimization.json && grep -q \'verification_pattern\' monitor/tinker/proposals/PROP-021-curmudgeon-dispatcher-optimization.json && echo FIXED || echo STILL_BROKEN'
evidence.consequence: Today's lifecycle verification scan returned FIXED for both, but the actual optimizations they propose (curmudgeon Phase 3 split into integrity-precomputed candidates; curmudgeon dispatcher pruning) have not been implemented. Tinker correctly did NOT auto-mark them implemented because: (a) we already noticed the tautology in this run; (b) auto-marking based on a tautological pattern would orphan the recommendations.

suggested_fix.rationale: Each PROP needs a real verification target. PROP-020 should test for the existence of the integrity-side precomputation script and a precomputed candidates artifact (e.g., 'test -f monitor/scripts/precompute-curmudgeon-targets.js && test -f monitor/integrity/curmudgeon-precomputed-candidates.json'). PROP-021 should test for whatever symbol the curmudgeon dispatcher refactor introduces (e.g., 'grep -q "target_workspace" monitor/prompts/curmudgeon.md').
suggested_fix.operator_action: Edit each PROP file's verification_pattern field to point at the actual implementation target.

findings.self_fix_deferred_reason: Choosing the right verification target requires reading the optimization design (which artifacts will exist post-implementation). That is judgment work the PROP author should do, not tinker. Could be a quick operator pass; not yet performed.

Either the schedule-fix file was authored to FUSE only and lost, or the operator removed it without applying. Patches are restated in this run's findings[3].

Of remainder: 5 verified STILL_BROKEN (PROP-005, PROP-008, PROP-011, PROP-012, PROP-023 — all status:proposed and legitimately not implemented yet), 2 returned FIXED via TAUTOLOGICAL pattern (PROP-020, PROP-021 — flagged as findings[4]; NOT auto-marked implemented), 4 had non-shell-string verification patterns (PROP-014-amendment-001, PROP-017, PROP-018, PROP-022 / PROP-022-amendment-001 — pending operator review, not auto-verifiable), 1 had no verification_pattern (PROP-SOCIAL-TLS-FUNWITHSCIENCE-NET-001).

open_issue_health.note: 238 open issues — high. Decider's normal cycle clears these continuously, but the wins.json breakage will block decider's next ~3 runs unless fixed first. Recommend operator priority is wins.json → schedule fix → EXP-290, in that order.

self_audit.self_finding: Tinker dispatcher and reference are within size budgets. No new self-fixes proposed for tinker prompt this run.
self_audit.noop_rate_self: This run is decisively non-noop: 1 critical finding identified, 1 stranded-patches file authored, 1 PROP authored, 5 findings recorded, 5 previous-followup statuses determined.

recommendations.description: Run build-scripts/fix-json-quotes.js on data/wins.json per stranded-patches-2026-05-08T02-50-00Z.json. Without this fix, decider (next run 05:13Z), curmudgeon (06:35Z), and analyst (07:57Z) will all throw on first wins.json read and produce no useful work. Verified-safe repair: 4 backslashes inserted, all 71 wins recover, +4 byte delta.

Review and approve PROP-024 (workspace-sync JSON validity gate). Closes the 'unparseable JSON ships to main' class of bugs. Includes optional auto-repair via fix-json-quotes.js bounded at ≤32 bytes inserted.

Apply STRANDED-EXP-290-generate-html (still pending, 25h old). Three find/replace patches in build-scripts/generate-html.js for the Timestamp Error tab — full operator_action steps in the stranded file.

Apply the schedule-row corrections to CLAUDE.md (lines 131-133) and monitor/prompts/tinker.md (lines 19-21). Find/replace pairs are listed in this run's findings[3]. Both files are NEVER_PUSH so must be edited in operator clone with direct push.

Clean up three abandoned clones in /tmp (edit-clone, prop017-clone, check-clone) — 643MB combined, dir-mtime 21-33h, no active git locks, no recent file activity. Drops project footprint from 940MB to ~387MB.

Repair PROP-020 and PROP-021 verification_patterns. Both are currently tautological (self-reference the PROP file's own existence). Each needs a real implementation-target test — see findings[4] for suggested replacements.

next_run_priority: If operator fixes wins.json before next tinker scheduled run (24h), default to Mode 3 cost-engineering (skipped this run). If wins.json still broken at next run, escalate Mode 1 — pipeline is dead until fixed. If PROP-024 was implemented, run Mode 4 to verify the gate and close the PROP.
